text: If, however, despite of the drainage with the chest tube, there is persistent purulent material that goes out, then we might have no other choice but to do a torcostomy window or an Alloser flap.
This one shows an Alloser flap allowing continuous drainage of the fluid for the patient.
That allows the chest cavity to drain properly and eventually dry up.
So this is one of our patients that we did.
So you see it's a very big tumor.
This was discovered by an incidental finding on routine x-ray.
That is the attachment to the visceral pleura.
